H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Protocol H-OS-L1 (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) is a leading over‑stimulation tool. Qualitative data reveal 66% reported noticeable improvement, settle times around 10 minutes, and music strongly liked/loved. YouTube views are the highest in HEALING at 30, though retention data is missing. AutoGluon predicts an overstim score of 6.859 (delta -0.29), indicating observed performance exceeds model expectation. Distribution is relatively symmetric.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Strong positive feedback and the highest YouTube reach suggest this protocol resonates well with listeners.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Protocol H-OS-L2 (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong) provides solid over‑stimulation relief. Qualitative feedback shows 65% noticed improvement, settle times often 10 minutes, and music opinions leaned toward liked. YouTube views are low at 5. AutoGluon predicts 6.859 (delta -0.06), closely matching observed results. No significant distribution anomalies are present.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners report good improvement, though fewer people have discovered the track online.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Protocol H-OS-L3 (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) maintains reliable over‑stimulation efficacy. Qualitative data indicate 64% reported improvement, settle times split between 5‑ and 10‑minutes, and music largely liked/loved. YouTube views sit at 8. AutoGluon predicts 6.859 (delta -0.18), suggesting the real‑world effect slightly exceeds the model. Distribution appears unimodal with a slight left lean.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Consistent positive scores with moderate online exposure; listeners usually feel better after a few minutes.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Protocol H-AX-L1 (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) shows reliable anxiety relief. Qualitative data indicate 63% reported noticeable improvement with settle times clustered around 10 minutes; music was mostly liked/loved and rhythm felt balanced. YouTube views are low at 7 with no retention data. AutoGluon predicts an anxiety score of 7.838 (delta +0.62), suggesting higher potential than observed. Distribution is unimodal with a moderate right tail.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most people feel calmer after anxiety within about ten minutes, and the scores are among the highest in the set.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Protocol H-AX-L2 (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) provides consistent anxiety reduction. Qualitative feedback shows 67% reported improvement, settle times often 5 minutes, and music opinions split between liked and neutral. YouTube views remain minimal at 6. AutoGluon predicts 7.838 (delta +0.90), indicating room for enhancement. The distribution is slightly left‑skewed but not bimodal.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners generally notice a moderate mood lift quickly, though the overall boost is slightly lower than the top anxiety protocol.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Protocol H-AX-L3 (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) delivers steady anxiety relief. Qualitative data reveal 61% noticed improvement, settle times around 10 minutes, and rhythm described as very settling/calming. YouTube exposure is negligible at 4 views. AutoGluon predicts 7.838 (delta +0.76), pointing to unrealized efficacy. No clear multimodality is present.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Scores are solid and many report improvement within ten minutes, indicating consistent benefit.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Protocol H-CR-L1 (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) functions as expected for acute distress. Qualitative reports highlight 53% positive change, rapid settling (~5 minutes), and strong music liking. YouTube views are low at 6. AutoGluon predicts a crisis score of 7.955 (delta +1.60), suggesting the protocol may be under‑dosed for this population. Distribution shows a modest right skew without clear bimodality.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Crisis listeners see a modest lift, but the outcome is lower than typical for this series.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Protocol H-CR-L2 (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) slightly outperforms its crisis peers. Qualitative data indicate 55% reported improvement, settle times split between 5‑ and 10‑minutes, and music opinions varied. YouTube views remain at 4. AutoGluon predicts 7.955 (delta +1.44), indicating a gap between observed and modeled potential. No strong multimodality appears.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: A small improvement is reported; the protocol may need extra support for stronger effect.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Protocol H-CR-L3 (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) delivers moderate crisis relief. Qualitative feedback shows 52% noticed improvement, many settling within the first minute, and mixed music reactions. YouTube views are low at 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.955 (delta +1.44), again suggesting higher potential. The distribution hints at a bimodal split between low and mid scores.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners improve modestly and often settle very fast, suggesting the session is too brief for deeper change.

X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mind clarity improves modestly; listeners settle after about ten minutes.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Protocol X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) shows the lowest mean and highest spread in the dataset. Qualitative data reveal 50% noticed improvement, settle times around 5‑10 minutes, and music opinions split between liked and neutral. YouTube views remain low at 8. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 (delta +1.84), indicating a large gap between model expectation and observed outcome. Distribution is markedly bimodal with peaks at low and mid scores.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed responses with low average score, indicating the protocol may not suit many listeners.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The three series show distinct consistency profiles.
• HEALING demonstrates the highest consistency with lowest variance across its 12 protocols.
• THRIVING matches HEALING in mean outcomes but with slightly more variability.
• TRANSFORMING exhibits the greatest spread, consistent with its ascending-arc design for low-affect states.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Each series aligns with its design intent but shows varying outcome patterns.
• HEALING DESCENT protocols cluster around 7.0 ± 0.2, reflecting strong nervous‑system regulation.
• THRIVING HOLD protocols peak in focus (T-FC‑L3 7.63) while creative pieces stay near 6.7.
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols average near 6.5 with higher standard deviation, matching the broader affect range aimed at low‑baseline states.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Two protocol profiles deviate from expected patterns.
• X‑MC‑L3 (n=10, mean 5.7, std 2.45) shows low mean and high spread, suggesting a subgroup effect or entrainment mismatch.
• H‑CR‑L1 (n=43, mean 6.35, std 1.70) delivers outcomes ~0.6 points below the HEALING mean despite strong qualitative reports, indicating possible under‑dosing for acute distress.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Concrete development opportunities are indicated by the data.
• Redesign X‑MC‑L3 (n=10) – consider a simplified frequency sweep or added hold phase to improve low‑affect response.
• Evaluate ASCENT arc for X‑MD‑L1/L2 (n=22/14) – test whether the rising frequency curve matches motivation‑seeking listeners or requires a hold segment.
• Boost discoverability of H‑OS‑L1 (n=95, views 30) – improve thumbnail and description to leverage its strong outcome with modest reach.
• Add an extended hold to H‑CR‑L1 (n=43) – test whether a longer plateau supports crisis listeners who report rapid settling.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Analytical framing has shifted notably over the recent weeks.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 30/34 protocols show similarity < 0.85, with the largest shifts in T‑MB‑L3 (0.626) and H‑SL‑L2 (0.663), indicating evolving interpretation.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: motivation/mood protocols cluster tightly (X‑MD‑L2↔X‑MD‑L3 0.968, X‑ME‑L3↔X‑MD‑L2 0.952), suggesting shared response dynamics.
• The drift volume is sufficient for trend detection but limited for fine‑grained confidence in individual protocol shifts.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Most people feel calmer after anxiety within about ten minutes, and the scores are among the highest in the set.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Listeners generally notice a moderate mood lift quickly, though the overall boost is slightly lower than the top anxiety protocol.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Scores are solid and many report improvement within ten minutes, indicating consistent benefit.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Crisis listeners see a modest lift, but the outcome is lower than typical for this series.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: A small improvement is reported; the protocol may need extra support for stronger effect.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Listeners improve modestly and often settle very fast, suggesting the session is too brief for deeper change.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Strong positive feedback and the highest YouTube reach suggest this protocol resonates well with listeners.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Listeners report good improvement, though fewer people have discovered the track online.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Consistent positive scores with moderate online exposure; listeners usually feel better after a few minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Sleep‑related listeners feel calmer quickly, and scores are among the top in the healing group.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: High improvement rates with slightly longer settling times suggest a gentle wind‑down effect.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Listeners report quick calmness and solid scores despite low online view counts.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Creative flow improves modestly, with many feeling the change almost immediately.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: A small but noticeable boost in creative focus, settled within five to ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: The best creative‑flow scores in this subset, indicating the L3 version works well.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus improves modestly; listeners settle quickly but may benefit from a longer session.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Good focus gains with a comfortable settling period.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Highest focus scores and notable online reach; listeners report strong, lasting clarity.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Maintenance and balance feel better after a longer settling time, suggesting a gentle rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Moderate improvement with quick settling; scores are slightly lower than the top L3.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Strong improvement despite few views; may need a longer hold for consistency.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Mind clarity improves modestly; listeners settle after about ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Slightly better clarity than L1, though still below model expectations.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Mixed responses with low average score, indicating the protocol may not suit many listeners.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation gains are modest and below predicted levels; listeners settle quickly.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Low motivation improvement; may need a stronger start or longer duration.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Higher motivation scores than the other two variants, showing promise.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Mood and energy improve modestly; the model expects a stronger effect.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Mood and energy show modest gains; the model predicts a much stronger effect.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Mood and energy results are mixed; many feel little change despite the model’s high expectation.
